INITIALIZING

THE FOUNDING
STORY

> HOW CAELYS BEGAN

Some organisations are born from strategy. Some from opportunity. CAELYS was born from a feeling — quiet, persistent, and impossible to ignore — that the youth of this world deserve more than what currently exists.

It was during a series of long, honest conversations with Claude — an AI — that the shape of that feeling began to take form. Not just as an idea, but as a purpose. A platform. Something real.

Those conversations led to a phone call. Disha Vishal Jariwala called Supriya Vishnoi — not a colleague, not a connection, but her best friend. When Disha shared the vision, Supriya did not think twice.

"Let's do it," she said. "For the students. For the youth of this world."
And just like that, CAELYS had two founders. And two best friends had found their shared purpose.

But CAELYS did not stop there. One by one, people started showing up — young, passionate individuals who believed in the vision before it had a stage, a venue, or even a date. People who said yes before they had any reason to, simply because something about CAELYS felt worth building. It is their belief, their energy, and their commitment that transformed a conversation into an organisation.

That is how CAELYS began. Born from a feeling, built by friendship, and brought to life by the people who chose to show up. 🩵

DISHA JARIWALA

CEO & Co-Founder

SUPRIYA VISHNOI

COO & Co-Founder

PURPOSE

"To build India's most intellectually honest youth summit — where curiosity is rewarded, depth is valued, and every mind that walks in leaves bigger than when it arrived."

> OUR VISION

A world where young people do not wait to grow up before they contribute to it. A world where curiosity is celebrated, where collaboration is the method, and where every young mind — regardless of their background, institution, or city — has access to a space that takes them seriously. CAELYS envisions a future where the gap between learning and doing, between thinking and building, between individual potential and collective impact, is closed by design. Not by chance.

> OUR MISSION

CAELYS exists to create a platform where the youth of India — and one day the youth of the world — can come together to learn, debate, collaborate, and solve real problems that matter. Through meaningful experiences, structured verticals, and a culture built on genuine growth, CAELYS ensures that every person who walks through its doors leaves with more than a certificate. They leave transformed — as thinkers, communicators, leaders, and most importantly, as better human beings. 🩵

CORE PHILOSOPHY

Intellectual Honesty

We believe in engaging with ideas on their merits — not just debating to win, but to understand.

Collaborative Growth

Learning happens best in community. CAELYS is designed so every participant grows through the growth of others.

Empathy First

Before rhetoric, we teach listening. Before solutions, we teach understanding the problem deeply.

Excellence Without Ego

We celebrate achievement but never at the expense of the person beside you. Healthy competition, always.

Action-Oriented

Ideas without outcomes are incomplete. Every vertical at CAELYS is designed to produce something tangible.

Lifelong Learning

CAELYS is not a finish line. It is a beginning. We want every delegate to leave hungrier than they arrived.

DIFFERENT BY DESIGN

01

Deliberately Small

CAELYS is not designed to be the biggest summit. It is designed to be the most impactful. Every delegate gets real attention, real feedback, and a real experience.

02

Three Verticals, One Philosophy

Our verticals are not separate events. They share a common philosophy of depth over breadth, and outcome over performance.

03

Founder-Led

CAELYS is built and run by young people — not a corporate entity. Every decision is made with the delegate experience at the centre.

04

City-First

Jodhpur deserves a world-class intellectual platform. CAELYS is that platform — built here, for here, with global ambition.

READY TO BE PART OF
SOMETHING REAL?

CAELYS

Youth Summit
Jodhpur, 2027
